title: Shor's Algorithm

theory.introduction: Shor's Algorithm is a quantum algorithm used to efficiently factor large integers. It demonstrates exponential speedup compared to the best known classical factoring algorithms and has major implications for cryptography, especially RSA encryption.
key_points: Efficient integer factorization using quantum computation
Uses Quantum Phase Estimation as a core subroutine
Transforms factoring problem into period finding problem
Breaks RSA cryptography if large-scale quantum computers exist

intuition.description: Factoring large numbers is difficult classically. Shor's algorithm converts factoring into a periodicity detection problem, which quantum computers can solve efficiently using interference.
intuition.core_idea: Find the period of modular exponentiation function using quantum phase estimation.

mathematical_representation.requirement: r must be even and a^(r/2) ≠ -1 mod N

sections.title: Problem Definition
content: Given a composite number N, find its prime factors
Classical factoring becomes inefficient for very large numbers

sections.title: Reduction to Period Finding
content: Choose a random integer a such that gcd(a,N)=1
Find the period r such that f(x+r)=f(x)
Period r reveals factors of N

sections.title: Quantum Advantage
content: Quantum parallelism evaluates many inputs simultaneously
Quantum interference amplifies correct period values

sections.title: Classical Post Processing
content: Once period r is found, compute gcd(a^(r/2) ± 1, N)
High probability of obtaining non-trivial factors

registers.input_register: Superposition of values from 0 to Q-1

steps.description: Pick random integer a such that gcd(a,N)=1
Compute f(x)=a^x mod N using modular exponentiation circuit
Compute gcd(a^(r/2) ± 1, N) to find factors

circuit_examples.name: Period Finding Circuit
circuit_examples.description: Core quantum circuit for estimating period
circuit_pattern: Apply H gates to input register

summary: Shor's algorithm efficiently factors integers by converting factoring into a period finding problem solved using Quantum Fourier Transform.

simulation.expected_result: Measurement reveals information about the period r
try_this: Increase number of counting qubits for better precision

quiz.question: What computational problem does Shor's algorithm solve?
Which quantum subroutine is essential for Shor's algorithm?
What mathematical concept allows factoring using period finding?
Why is Shor's algorithm important?
options: It breaks widely used cryptographic systems
quiz.answer: It breaks widely used cryptographic systems
